methods.name: Stage-Progression

data.narrative:
Dexterity is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Samir Menon, Robert Sun, and four co-founders, currently valued at $1.65B post-money after raising $291M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of nine private companies and one public company (ticker 277810.KQ) in robotics and manufacturing, including peers such as Bedrock Robotics, Hadrian, and Nimble Robotics.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage transitions, yielding an implied valuation of $3.1B. The peer-median method examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B (Series B), Hadrian at $1.6B (Series C), and Nimble Robotics at $1.0B (Series C)—producing a median implied valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.8B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B to $3.6B, with a median of $2.4B. The wide spread reflects tension between the peer-median method anchored to the current post-money and the stage-progression method assuming above-market momentum. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiplier for this company's growth trajectory relative to peers and the representativeness of the three comparable companies selected.
